content:
# Hydraulic Conductivity

**Summary**: A measure of the ease with which a fluid can move through pore spaces or fractures.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020]] analyzed hydraulic-conductivity to determine how it varies between eastern and western parts of the aquifer.

- [[2026-fatima|Fatima, 2026]] measured hydraulic conductivity for materials U, L, and D.

## Scientific Context

- [[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020]] Determines the ability of the aquifer to supply water and recharge from wadis.

- [[2026-fatima|Fatima, 2026]] measured hydraulic conductivity for materials U, L, and D.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020]] Comparison of values between eastern and western sections.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Hydraulic conductivity values are high westward and low in the eastern side of the study area. ([[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020, p. 20]])

- Measured values were 3.2 m/day for material L and 4.8 m/day for material D. ([[2026-fatima|Fatima, 2026, p. 47]])
